description:
You are a data-analyst who wants to compare the wall-clock runtime of two different
    approaches for aggregating the quarterly sales data that lives under
    /home/user/data/.  
    Three CSV files already exist:

      • /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv  
      • /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv  
      • /home/user/data/sales_q3.csv

    They all share the exact same column layout (including the header row):

        Region,Product,Units,UnitPrice

    1.  Compute the **total revenue per Region** across the three quarters.
        Revenue for a row = Units × UnitPrice (both columns are numeric).

    2.  Write the result to /home/user/output/aggregated_sales.csv.
        Required format (including capitalisation, order, number of decimals,
        and the final newline character):

            Region,TotalRevenue
            East,418.00
            North,580.50
            South,269.00
            West,157.00

        Notes  
        • The file must contain exactly five lines (1 header + 4 data lines).  
        • Use a comma as the sole delimiter.  
        • Values in the ‘TotalRevenue’ column must always have exactly two
          digits after the decimal point.  
        • Lines must end with Unix LF ( \n ).  

    3.  Benchmark two different aggregation strategies:

        a. a «single-thread»/sequential strategy  
        b. a «parallel» strategy (e.g. using GNU parallel, the & operator,
           xargs -P, etc.)

        Measure the wall-clock runtime of each strategy with the `/usr/bin/time
        -f "%e"` tool (or an equivalent technique that records only the elapsed
        seconds).

    4.  Create the log file /home/user/output/runtime_comparison.log with **exactly
        three lines**, in the order shown below:

            single_thread_elapsed: <N.NN>
            parallel_elapsed: <N.NN>
            speedup: <N.NN>x

        • <N.NN> stands for any positive decimal number with at least one digit
          before and exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
        • No extra whitespace is allowed before or after the colon.  
        • The trailing “x” in the third line is mandatory.  
        • End the file with a single newline character.  

    5.  When you are finished, both files
        (/home/user/output/aggregated_sales.csv and
        /home/user/output/runtime_comparison.log) must exist and comply with the
        formats given above so that an automated test suite can validate them.

    You do not have root privileges, so work entirely inside /home/user/.
    Feel free to create temporary helper scripts or directories, but only the
    two output files defined above will be checked by the grader.
truth:
Pre-existing files and their exact contents:

    1. /home/user/data/sales_q1.csv  
       ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │Region,Product,Units,UnitPrice          │
       │North,Widget,10,20.5                    │
       │South,Gadget,5,15                       │
       │East,Widget,7,20.5                      │
       │North,Gadget,3,15                       │
       └────────────────────────────────────────┘

    2. /home/user/data/sales_q2.csv  
       ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │Region,Product,Units,UnitPrice          │
       │South,Widget,8,20.5                     │
       │East,Gadget,6,15                        │
       │West,Widget,4,20.5                      │
       │North,Widget,11,20.5                    │
       └────────────────────────────────────────┘

    3. /home/user/data/sales_q3.csv  
       ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │Region,Product,Units,UnitPrice          │
       │East,Widget,9,20.5                      │
       │South,Gadget,2,15                       │
       │West,Gadget,5,15                        │
       │North,Gadget,7,15                       │
       └────────────────────────────────────────┘

    Expected aggregation result:

        /home/user/output/aggregated_sales.csv  
            Region,TotalRevenue\n
            East,418.00\n
            North,580.50\n
            South,269.00\n
            West,157.00\n

        (Note: “\n” marks a single LF byte.)

    Runtime-log verification pattern:

        /home/user/output/runtime_comparison.log must match the
        Perl-compatible-regular-expression (PCRE):

            \Asingle_thread_elapsed:\s[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}\n
            parallel_elapsed:\s[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}\n
            speedup:\s[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}x\n\z

    The test harness will load the file and validate it against that regex
    rather than fixed numbers, so any correct timings are acceptable as long as
    they honour the required formatting.

    Directory /home/user/output/ is guaranteed to exist and be writable by the
    agent before the task starts.
